title: Myrsine miquelii
taxonomy: Myrsine miquelii is classified within the family Myrsinaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. This species is further classified under the genus Myrsine, which consists of various species of shrubs and small trees. The kingdom to which Myrsine miquelii belongs is Plantae, which includes all types of plants. Myrsine miquelii is also classified under the division Magnoliophyta, which is a division of flowering plants. The full classification of Myrsine miquelii is therefore: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Magnoliophyta, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Ericales, Family: Myrsinaceae, Genus: Myrsine, Species: M. miquelii.

intro: Myrsine miquelii, also known as Miquel's myrsine or Miquel's round-leaf myrsine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Taiwan, to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Myrsine miquelii include iridoids, phenolic compounds, and alkaloids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, Myrsine miquelii is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as to promote sweating and relieve pain.
